title: dianthus ohridanus
intro: Dianthus ohridanus, also known as the Ohrid pink or Macedonian pink, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant's flowers are pink or white and have a strong fragrance, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The essential oil extracted from the flowers is known to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oil preparations, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
